rulings.Case Title: CORNELIUS MURPHY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel The Depositions Of Defendant Svi Corporation'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 31, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of defendant SVI Corporation¿s PMK and COR and production of documents: If all issues raised by this motion have not been resolved before then, by 4:30pm on August 30 the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, which issues remain in dispute and then appear at the hearing on August 31. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MATTIE MCFALL et al VS. ARVIN MERITOR, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Bentley Motors, Inc., Mercedes-Benz Usa Llc, Volkswagen Group Of America, Inc.¿S Motion For Order Compelling Mattie Mcfall To Provide A Further Response To Document Requests And Produce All Responsive Materials And Things; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 31, 2011, line 1. Defendants Bentley Motors, Inc., Mercedes-Benz USA LLC, Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.¿s motion for order compelling Mattie McFall to provide a further response to document requests and produce all responsive materials and things, request for monetary sanctions: the parties are required to appear in Dept. 504 at 9:30am, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding, for a compliance hearing. Based on defendant¿s supplemental papers, plaintiffs failed to provide further responses to the outstanding requests and failed to produce documents by the court-ordered date of July 21. Unless plaintiffs can show that they acted with substantial justification, the court will impose sanctions against plaintiffs¿ counsel. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Plaintiff Interrogatories To Defendants
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 31, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiff¿s motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories from defendant Metallo Gasket Co. is granted. Defendant has thirty days to provide verified responses. General Order 129 requires that defendant provide such responses. Plaintiff¿s request that the hearing on any dispositive motions filed by defendant in asbestos cases before this court be stayed until 60 days after defendant has provided verified responses is granted. As long as GO 129 remain in effect and has not been invalidated, it must be complied with. Treating defendant¿s arguments as grounds why GO 127 should be invalidated as to it, those arguments lack merit due to GO 129¿s provisions that allow a new defendant to seek relief, which defendant did not do. Plaintiff¿s request for sanctions is denied. While a close call, defendant¿s arguments were not wholly lacking in substantial justification. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder Of Plaintiffs Represented By Waters, Kraus & Paul In Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel The Annual Update To Response To General Order No. 129 Standard Plaintiffs' Interrogatories From Defendant Metallo Gasket Company, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, August 31, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Waters, Kraus & Paul¿s joinder in plaintiff¿s motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories to defendants from defendant Metallo Gasket Co. is off calendar as moot given the ruling on the underlying motion. = (220/HEK)
